headline: SLOP WATCH ISSUED OVER THE FACEBOOK DELTA AS VIRAL-ARITHMETIC FRONT MOVES IN, DEMANDING THAT NINETY-NINE PERCENT OF RESIDENTS FAIL A PROBLEM WITH NO CORRECT ANSWER
advisory: Following the extended pyrotechnic and patriotism systems of the holiday weekend, the atmosphere has cooled into a lower-energy but persistent condition. A slow-moving mass of engagement-bait arithmetic — deliberately ambiguous order-of-operations puzzles captioned as intelligence tests — is expected to settle across comment sections through Sunday evening. Residents are advised that the correct answer is not the goal; the argument is the goal. Do not attempt to resolve the equation, as this only strengthens the system.

regional_outlook.conditions: Heavy accumulation of '99% will get this WRONG!' arithmetic imagery, with scattered fruit-emoji algebra developing after noon. Comment sections flooding to record depth.
Same equations arriving pre-argued, wrapped in confident wrong answers and reply-guy turbulence. Visibility near zero by afternoon.
Quiet now, but a Sunday-evening 'here's what the weekend taught me about leadership' system is organizing offshore and will make landfall by Monday morning.
Residual synthetic 'answer explained' pages backfilling the arithmetic front, each confidently citing a different total. Groundwater unreliable.
Calm and warm, with drifting AI-generated 'cozy Sunday reset' imagery depicting kitchens that violate load-bearing physics.

specimen.status: Abundant; year-round resident, peaks on low-effort weekends.
specimen.habitat: Comment-driven feeds, particularly the Facebook Delta and X Badlands. Prefers still water where an argument can pool.
specimen.field_marks: A bright, badly-kerned image bearing an equation such as 6 ÷ 2(1+2) engineered for maximum notation ambiguity, garnished with 'Only a GENIUS can solve this!' and a statistically invented failure rate. Never shows its work.
specimen.call: 98% GOT THIS WRONG!! Comment your answer below before you scroll — no calculators!!
specimen.confusion_species: Easily mistaken for a genuine brain teaser posted by a math teacher. Distinguish by intent: a real puzzle wants you to learn something and has one defensible answer; the Impossible Arithmetic wants you to fight a stranger and has none. If the caption cites a percentage it cannot possibly have measured, you have found the slop.

spotter_tip: If solving the problem correctly would end the conversation, the post was never about the problem. Report the failure-rate statistic to yourself and move on.
tomorrow: Arithmetic front weakens overnight; expect the LinkedIn Plateau motivational system to intensify by Monday sunrise.
